nodes.label: Malaria
nodes.description: A life-threatening disease spread to humans by some types of mosquitoes, mostly found in tropical countries.

nodes.label: Plasmodium
nodes.description: A genus of parasites that cause malaria in humans.

nodes.label: P. falciparum
nodes.description: The deadliest malaria parasite and the most prevalent on the African continent.

nodes.label: P. vivax
nodes.description: The dominant malaria parasite in most countries outside of sub-Saharan Africa.

nodes.label: P. malariae
nodes.description: A malaria species which can infect humans.

nodes.label: P. ovale
nodes.description: A malaria species which can infect humans.

nodes.label: P. knowlesi
nodes.description: A malaria species which can infect humans.

nodes.label: Female Anopheles mosquitoes
nodes.description: Mosquitoes through whose bites malaria mostly spreads to people.

nodes.label: Anopheles stephensi
nodes.description: An invasive mosquito species that thrives in urban settings, endures high temperatures, and is resistant to many public health insecticides.

nodes.label: Blood transfusion
nodes.description: A route of malaria transmission.

nodes.label: Contaminated needles
nodes.description: A route of malaria transmission.

nodes.label: Fever
nodes.description: A common early mild symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Chills
nodes.description: A common early mild symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Headache
nodes.description: A common early mild symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Fatigue
nodes.description: A severe symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Impaired consciousness
nodes.description: A severe symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Multiple convulsions
nodes.description: A severe symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Difficulty breathing
nodes.description: A severe symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Dark or bloody urine
nodes.description: A severe symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Jaundice
aliases: Yellowing of the eyes and skin
nodes.description: A severe symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Abnormal bleeding
nodes.description: A severe symptom of malaria.

nodes.label: Premature delivery
nodes.description: A complication caused by malaria infection during pregnancy.

nodes.label: Low birth weight
nodes.description: A complication caused by malaria infection during pregnancy.

nodes.label: Infants
nodes.description: A high-risk population group for severe malaria infection.

nodes.label: Children under 5 years
nodes.description: A high-risk population group for severe malaria infection.

nodes.label: Pregnant women and girls
nodes.description: A high-risk population group for severe malaria infection.

nodes.label: Travellers
nodes.description: A high-risk population group for severe malaria infection when visiting endemic areas.

nodes.label: People with HIV or AIDS
nodes.description: A high-risk population group for severe malaria infection.

nodes.label: Vulnerable populations
nodes.description: Populations targeted for preventive chemotherapies.

nodes.label: School-aged children
nodes.description: A population segment receiving target interventions.

nodes.label: Avoiding mosquito bites
nodes.description: A baseline preventive behavior against malaria transmission.

nodes.label: Mosquito nets
nodes.description: Use of nets when sleeping in places where malaria is present.

nodes.label: Mosquito repellents
nodes.description: Repellents used after dusk to prevent mosquito bites.

nodes.label: Coils and vaporizers
nodes.description: Tools to lower the risk of getting malaria.

nodes.label: Protective clothing
nodes.description: Clothing worn to avoid mosquito bites.

nodes.label: Window screens
nodes.description: Screens used to block mosquitoes.

nodes.label: Vector control
nodes.description: A vital component of malaria control and elimination strategies highly effective in preventing infection.

nodes.label: Insecticide-treated nets
nodes.description: A core vector control intervention.

nodes.label: Indoor residual spraying
nodes.description: A core vector control intervention.

nodes.label: Chemoprophylaxis
nodes.description: Medicines taken before travelling to malaria endemic areas to prevent infection.

nodes.label: Preventive chemotherapy
nodes.description: The use of medicines, either alone or in combination, to prevent malaria infections and their consequences in vulnerable populations.

nodes.label: Perennial malaria chemoprevention
nodes.description: A strategy included under preventive chemotherapy.

nodes.label: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention
nodes.description: A strategy included under preventive chemotherapy.

nodes.label: Intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy
nodes.description: A strategy included under preventive chemotherapy.

nodes.label: Intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in school-aged children
nodes.description: A strategy included under preventive chemotherapy.

nodes.label: Post-discharge malaria chemoprevention
nodes.description: A strategy included under preventive chemotherapy.

nodes.label: Mass drug administration
nodes.description: A strategy included under preventive chemotherapy.

nodes.label: RTS,S/AS01
nodes.description: A malaria vaccine recommended by WHO since October 2021 for children in areas with moderate to high P. falciparum transmission.

nodes.label: R21/Matrix-M
nodes.description: A second safe and effective malaria vaccine recommended by WHO in October 2023.

nodes.label: Parasite-based diagnostic testing
nodes.description: Testing recommended by WHO to confirm all suspected cases of malaria.

nodes.label: Microscopy
nodes.description: A form of parasite-based diagnostic testing.

nodes.label: Rapid diagnostic test
nodes.description: A form of parasite-based diagnostic testing that usually targets HRP2 and HRP3 proteins.

nodes.label: Artemisinin-based combination therapy
nodes.description: The most effective treatment for P. falciparum malaria.

nodes.label: Chloroquine
nodes.description: Medicine recommended for treatment of infection with P. vivax only where it is still sensitive.

nodes.label: Primaquine
nodes.description: Medicine added to prevent relapses of infection with P. vivax and P. ovale.

nodes.label: Malaria surveillance
nodes.description: Continuous and systematic collection, analysis and interpretation of malaria-related data.

nodes.label: World Health Organization
nodes.description: The global directing and coordinating authority for health.

nodes.label: Global Malaria Programme
nodes.description: Coordinates WHO’s global efforts to control and eliminate malaria.

nodes.label: Mekong Malaria Elimination Programme
nodes.description: WHO program working on antimalarial drug resistance in the Greater Mekong subregion.

nodes.label: Global technical strategy for malaria 2016–2030
nodes.description: A technical framework updated in 2021 providing guidance for all malaria-endemic countries.

nodes.label: Strategy to curb antimalarial drug resistance in Africa
nodes.description: A strategy developed by WHO in 2022 to address drug resistance.

nodes.label: WHO African Region
nodes.description: The region that carries a disproportionately high share of the global malaria burden.

nodes.label: Greater Mekong subregion
nodes.description: Subregion where partial artemisinin resistance emerged.

nodes.label: sub-Saharan Africa
nodes.description: A geographic region heavily impacted by malaria.

nodes.label: Nigeria
nodes.description: Country accounting for 31.9% of all malaria deaths in the African region.

nodes.label: Democratic Republic of the Congo
nodes.description: Country accounting for 11.7% of all malaria deaths in the African region.

nodes.label: Niger
nodes.description: Country accounting for 6.1% of all malaria deaths in the African region.

nodes.label: Eritrea
nodes.description: Country with confirmed partial artemisinin resistance and mutated parasites prevalence >15%.

nodes.label: Rwanda
nodes.description: Country with confirmed partial artemisinin resistance.

nodes.label: Uganda
nodes.description: Country with confirmed partial artemisinin resistance.

nodes.label: United Republic of Tanzania
nodes.description: Country with confirmed partial artemisinin resistance.

nodes.label: Ethiopia
nodes.description: Country with suspected partial artemisinin resistance and mutated parasites prevalence >15%.

nodes.label: Namibia
nodes.description: Country with suspected partial artemisinin resistance.

nodes.label: Sudan
nodes.description: Country with suspected partial artemisinin resistance.

nodes.label: Zambia
nodes.description: Country with suspected partial artemisinin resistance.

nodes.label: Viet Nam
nodes.description: Country reporting mutated parasites for the first time in 2024.

nodes.label: Brazil
nodes.description: Country with mutated parasites prevalence exceeding 15%.

nodes.label: Djibouti
nodes.description: Country with mutated parasites prevalence exceeding 15%.

nodes.label: Nicaragua
nodes.description: Country with mutated parasites prevalence exceeding 15%.

nodes.label: Peru
nodes.description: Country with mutated parasites prevalence exceeding 15%.

nodes.label: Global Malaria Cases 2024
nodes.description: An estimated 282 million malaria cases globally in 2024.

nodes.label: Global Malaria Deaths 2024
nodes.description: An estimated 610 000 malaria deaths globally in 2024.

nodes.label: WHO African Region Cases Share 2024
nodes.description: The WHO African Region was home to 95% of malaria cases (265 million) in 2024.

nodes.label: WHO African Region Deaths Share 2024
nodes.description: The WHO African Region was home to 95% of malaria deaths (579 000) in 2024.

nodes.label: Children Under 5 Deaths Share in African Region
nodes.description: Children under 5 accounted for about 75% to 76% of all malaria deaths in the Region.

properties.context: expanding range over the last decade in 8 countries

properties.status: confirmed partial resistance in Eritrea, Rwanda, Uganda, United Republic of Tanzania; suspected in Ethiopia, Namibia, Sudan, Zambia

properties.reason: genetic mutations preventing HRP2/HRP3 expression escape detection
